nodes.x: I am the man that hath seen affliction by the rod of his wrath.
I, the Lord, have suffered the affliction to come upon them, wherewith they have been afflicted, in consequence of their transgressions;
But what is Persecution’s rod, but that Which shall be mado a blessing to the good ?
It may be that the Lord will look on mine affliction, and that the Lord will requite me good for his cursing this day.
I am afflicted very much: quicken me, O Lord, according unto thy word.
¶ Is it nothing to you, all ye that pass by? behold, and see if there be any sorrow like unto my sorrow, which is done unto me, wherewith the Lord hath afflicted me in the day of his fierce anger.
He hath led me, and brought me into darkness, but not into light.
He hath fenced up my way that I cannot pass, and he hath set darkness in my paths.
When his candle shined upon my head, and when by his light I walked through darkness;
And I, God, saw the light; and that light was good. And I, God, divided the light from the darkness.
Because I was not cut off before the darkness, neither hath he covered the darkness from my face.
Surely against me is he turned; he turneth his hand against me all the day.
Behold, he findeth occasions against me, he counteth me for his enemy,
He that is not with me is against me; and he that gathereth not with me scattereth abroad.
He that is not with me is against me: and he that gathereth not with me scattereth.
His left hand is under my head, and his right hand doth embrace me.
His left hand should be under my head, and his right hand should embrace me.
My flesh and my skin hath he made old; he hath broken my bones.
He hath also broken my teeth with gravel stones, he hath covered me with ashes.
From above hath he sent fire into my bones, and it prevaileth against them: he hath spread a net for my feet, he hath turned me back: he hath made me desolate and faint all the day.
He hath filled me with his love, even unto the consuming of my flesh.
Make me to hear joy and gladness; that the bones which thou hast broken may rejoice.
And I have led you forty years in the wilderness: your clothes are not waxen old upon you, and thy shoe is not waxen old upon thy foot.
He hath builded against me, and compassed me with gall and travail.
His archers compass me round about, he cleaveth my reins asunder, and doth not spare; he poureth out my gall upon the ground.
He hath inclosed my ways with hewn stone, he hath made my paths crooked.
And it came to pass that after they had bound me insomuch that I could not move, the compass, which had been prepared of the Lord, did cease to work.
They compassed me about also with words of hatred; and fought against me without a cause.
He hath set me in dark places, as they that be dead of old.
He hath led me, and brought me into darkness, but not into light.
For the enemy hath persecuted my soul; he hath smitten my life down to the ground; he hath made me to dwell in darkness, as those that have been long dead.
And he said, Behold now, I am old, I know not the day of my death:
Thou hast laid me in the lowest pit, in darkness, in the deeps.
He hath hedged me about, that I cannot get out: he hath made my chain heavy.
He hath turned aside my ways, and pulled me in pieces: he hath made me desolate.
And he hath made my mouth like a sharp sword; in the shadow of his hand hath he hid me, and made me a polished shaft; in his quiver hath he hid me;
He hath destroyed me on every side, and I am gone: and mine hope hath he removed like a tree.
Also when I cry and shout, he shutteth out my prayer.
In my distress I called upon the Lord, and cried to my God: and he did hear my voice out of his temple, and my cry did enter into his ears.
And he cried, saying, Jesus, thou Son of David, have mercy on me.
Hearken unto the voice of my cry, my King, and my God: for unto thee will I pray.
In my distress I called upon the Lord, and cried unto my God: he heard my voice out of his temple, and my cry came before him, even into his ears.
Yea, O God, and thou wast merciful unto me when I did cry unto thee in my field; when I did cry unto thee in my prayer, and thou didst hear me.
He hath inclosed my ways with hewn stone, he hath made my paths crooked.
He brought me up also out of an horrible pit, out of the miry clay, and set my feet upon a rock, and established my goings.
He hath hedged me about, that I cannot get out: he hath made my chain heavy.
He hath builded against me, and compassed me with gall and travail.
He was unto me as a bear lying in wait, and as a lion in secret places.

Then said he unto him, Because thou hast not obeyed the voice of the Lord, behold, as soon as thou art departed from me, a lion shall slay thee. And as soon as he was departed from him, a lion found him, and slew him.
And what he did unto you in the wilderness, until ye came into this place;
And the beast which I saw was like unto a leopard, and his feet were as the feet of a bear, and his mouth as the mouth of a lion: and the dragon gave him his power, and his seat, and great authority.
Like as a lion that is greedy of his prey, and as it were a young lion lurking in secret places.
He lieth in wait secretly as a lion in his den: he lieth in wait to catch the poor: he doth catch the poor, when he draweth him into his net.
He hath turned aside my ways, and pulled me in pieces: he hath made me desolate.
I was at ease, but he hath broken me asunder: he hath also taken me by my neck, and shaken me to pieces, and set me up for his mark.
But now he hath made me weary: thou hast made desolate all my company.
He hath bent his bow, and set me as a mark for the arrow.
He hath caused the arrows of his quiver to enter into my reins.
If he turn not, he will whet his sword; he hath bent his bow, and made it ready.
And he said unto his lad, Run, find out now the arrows which I shoot. And as the lad ran, he shot an arrow beyond him.
He hath caused the arrows of his quiver to enter into my reins.
He hath bent his bow, and set me as a mark for the arrow.
And he sent out arrows, and scattered them; lightning, and discomfited them.
I was a derision to all my people; and their song all the day.
And now am I their song, yea, I am their byword.

For a few moments after the song was finished I sat overpowered with a
feeling of indescribable peace. When I looked up the room was empty, my
friends had gone.
The lips of those that rose up against me, and their device against me all the day.
He hath filled me with bitterness, he hath made me drunken with wormwood.
He will not suffer me to take my breath, but filleth me with bitterness.
Ye who turn judgment to wormwood, and leave off righteousness in the earth,
But her end is bitter as wormwood, sharp as a twoedged sword.
He hath also broken my teeth with gravel stones, he hath covered me with ashes.
My flesh and my skin hath he made old; he hath broken my bones.
And he took him a potsherd to scrape himself withal; and he sat down among the ashes.
And thou hast removed my soul far off from peace: I forgat prosperity.
Behold, for peace I had great bitterness: but thou hast in love to my soul delivered it from the pit of corruption: for thou hast cast all my sins behind thy back.
And of thy mercy cut off mine enemies, and destroy all them that afflict my soul: for I am thy servant.
Thou shalt not seek their peace nor their prosperity all thy days for ever.

I say unto you, my brethren, that if you should render all the thanks and praise which your whole soul has power to possess, to that God who has created you, and has kept and preserved you, and has caused that ye should rejoice, and has granted that ye should live in peace one with another—
And thus shall ye say to him that liveth in prosperity, Peace be both to thee, and peace be to thine house, and peace be unto all that thou hast.
And I said, My strength and my hope is perished from the Lord:

And he said unto me, My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness. Most gladly therefore will I rather glory in my infirmities, that the power of Christ may rest upon me.

And said, O man greatly beloved, fear not: peace be unto thee, be strong, yea, be strong. And when he had spoken unto me, I was strengthened, and said, Let my lord speak; for thou hast strengthened me.

And I know that the Lord God will consecrate my prayers for the gain of my people. And the words which I have written in weakness will be made strong unto them; for it persuadeth them to do good; it maketh known unto them of their fathers; and it speaketh of Jesus, and persuadeth them to believe in him, and to endure to the end, which is life eternal.
Then I said, I have labored in vain, I have spent my strength for naught and in vain; surely my judgment is with the Lord, and my work with my God.
Then I said, I have laboured in vain, I have spent my strength for nought, and in vain: yet surely my judgment is with the Lord, and my work with my God.
Remembering mine affliction and my misery, the wormwood and the gall.

Mine eyes do fail with tears, my bowels are troubled, my liver is poured upon the earth, for the destruction of the daughter of my people; because the children and the sucklings swoon in the streets of the city.
Mine eye runneth down with rivers of water for the destruction of the daughter of my people.
I will remember the works of the Lord: surely I will remember thy wonders of old.
My soul hath them still in remembrance, and is humbled in me.
My soul hath kept thy testimonies; and I love them exceedingly.

And so great were their afflictions that every soul had cause to mourn; and they believed that it was the judgments of God sent upon them because of their wickedness and their abominations; therefore they were awakened to a remembrance of their duty.
This I recall to my mind, therefore have I hope.
Remember the word unto thy servant, upon which thou hast caused me to hope.

Or otherwise, can ye imagine yourselves brought before the tribunal of God with your souls filled with guilt and remorse, having a remembrance of all your guilt, yea, a perfect remembrance of all your wickedness, yea, a remembrance that ye have set at defiance the commandments of God?

had in everlasting remembrance; and we will exercise our faith that God will restore his health, that his voice may again be heard amongst us, though that is not possible at this time. We are gratified to know that
And they said, There is no hope: but we will walk after our own devices, and we will every one do the imagination of his evil heart.
¶ It is of the Lord’s mercies that we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not.
But though he cause grief, yet will he have compassion according to the multitude of his mercies.
Nevertheless for thy great mercies’ sake thou didst not utterly consume them, nor forsake them; for thou art a gracious and merciful God.
To the Lord our God belong mercies and forgivenesses, though we have rebelled against him;

And lo, he shall suffer temptations, and pain of body, hunger, thirst, and fatigue, even more than man can suffer, except it be unto death; for behold, blood cometh from every pore, so great shall be his anguish for the wickedness and the abominations of his people.
The Lord is good to all: and his tender mercies are over all his works.
They are new every morning: great is thy faithfulness.
To shew forth thy lovingkindness in the morning, and thy faithfulness every night,

which we are surrounded; and that the same God, who conferred- upon them such great privileges, “is the same yesterday to day and forever,” and is “no respecter of persons,” consequently is just as willing to hear and answer the prayers, and bestow as great blessings upon his children now, as in days gone by: therefore, beloved brethren, let us gird up our loins and Be faithful, knowing that the…
The Lord is my portion, saith my soul; therefore will I hope in him.
In God will I praise his word: in the Lord will I praise his word.

And what is it that ye shall hope for? Behold I say unto you that ye shall have hope through the atonement of Christ and the power of his resurrection, to be raised unto life eternal, and this because of your faith in him according to the promise.
My soul fainteth for thy salvation: but I hope in thy word.

his own, and build up his kingdom, and it will go forth from this time until the earth shall be covered with the knowledge of the Lord. That we may be found faithful and worthy to enjoy the fulness of the glory of his celestial kingdom is my prayer. Amen.
The Lord is good unto them that wait for him, to the soul that seeketh him.
The Lord is good, a strong hold in the day of trouble; and he knoweth them that trust in him.

¶ And therefore will the Lord wait, that he may be gracious unto you, and therefore will he be exalted, that he may have mercy upon you: for the Lord is a God of judgment: blessed are all they that wait for him.
And the Lord shall help them, and deliver them: he shall deliver them from the wicked, and save them, because they trust in him.
For the Lord is good; his mercy is everlasting; and his truth endureth to all generations.
Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the Lord.
It is good that a man should both hope and quietly wait for the salvation of the Lord.
For we through the Spirit wait for the hope of righteousness by faith.
For we are saved by hope: but hope that is seen is not hope: for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for?
Wherefore, if a man have faith he must needs have hope; for without faith there cannot be any hope.
¶ And it shall be said in that day, Lo, this is our God; we have waited for him, and he will save us: this is the Lord; we have waited for him, we will be glad and rejoice in his salvation.
The Lord is good unto them that wait for him, to the soul that seeketh him.
It is good for a man that he bear the yoke in his youth.
For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.

The yoke of my transgressions is bound by his hand: they are wreathed, and come up upon my neck: he hath made my strength to fall, the Lord hath delivered me into their hands, from whom I am not able to rise up.

And spake to them after the counsel of the young men, saying, My father made your yoke heavy, and I will add to your yoke: my father also chastised you with whips, but I will chastise you with scorpions.
I suppose therefore that this is good for the present distress, I say, that it is good for a man so to be.
He sitteth alone and keepeth silence, because he hath borne it upon him.
And he that shall swear by heaven, sweareth by the throne of God, and by him that sitteth thereon.
Because he hath despised the word of the Lord, and hath broken his commandment, that soul shall utterly be cut off; his iniquity shall be upon him.
Nevertheless he that standeth steadfast in his heart, having no necessity, but hath power over his own will, and hath so decreed in his heart that he will keep his virgin, doeth well.
For he that is entered into his rest, he also hath ceased from his own works, as God did from his.
If any thing be revealed to another that sitteth by, let the first hold his peace.
He putteth his mouth in the dust; if so be there may be hope.
Yet he shall perish for ever like his own dung: they which have seen him shall say, Where is he?
And they shall mock him, and shall scourge him, and shall spit upon him, and shall kill him: and the third day he shall rise again.
And he took him aside from the multitude, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spit, and touched his tongue;
He giveth his cheek to him that smiteth him: he is filled full with reproach.
Bless, Lord, his substance, and accept the work of his hands: smite through the loins of them that rise against him, and of them that hate him, that they rise not again.
He holdeth back the face of his throne, and spreadeth his cloud upon it.
¶ Hath he smitten him, as he smote those that smote him? or is he slain according to the slaughter of them that are slain by him?
For the Lord will not cast off for ever:
Will the Lord cast off for ever? and will he be favourable no more?
For the Lord will not cast off his people, neither will he forsake his inheritance.
The Lord is King for ever and ever: the heathen are perished out of his land.

who shall be cast off from the presence of the Lord; Yea, who shall be consigned to a state of endless misery, fulfilling the words which say: They that have done good, shall have everlasting life; and they that have done evil, shall have everlasting damnation. And thus it is. Amen.”
Awake, why sleepest thou, O Lord? arise, cast us not off for ever.
But though he cause grief, yet will he have compassion according to the multitude of his mercies.
¶ It is of the Lord’s mercies that we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not.
But he, being full of compassion, forgave their iniquity, and destroyed them not: yea, many a time turned he his anger away, and did not stir up all his wrath.
He will turn again, he will have compassion upon us; he will subdue our iniquities; and thou wilt cast all their sins into the depths of the sea.
Surely he has borne our griefs, and carried our sorrows; yet we did esteem him stricken, smitten of God, and afflicted.

And he will take upon him death, that he may loose the bands of death which bind his people; and he will take upon him their infirmities, that his bowels may be filled with mercy, according to the flesh, that he may know according to the flesh how to succor his people according to their infirmities.
For he doth not afflict willingly nor grieve the children of men.
Yet setteth he the poor on high from affliction, and maketh him families like a flock.
¶ Strive not with a man without cause, if he have done thee no harm.
For whatsoever soul it be that shall not be afflicted in that same day, he shall be cut off from among his people.
To him that is afflicted pity should be shewed from his friend; but he forsaketh the fear of the Almighty.
To crush under his feet all the prisoners of the earth,
To go into the clefts of the rocks, and into the tops of the ragged rocks, for fear of the Lord, and for the glory of his majesty, when he ariseth to shake terribly the earth.
And it came to pass that when he had raised the first from the ground, behold he stabbed the king to the heart; and he fell to the earth.
To bring back his soul from the pit, to be enlightened with the light of the living.
And they shall go into the holes of the rocks, and into the caves of the earth, for fear of the Lord, and for the glory of his majesty, when he ariseth to shake terribly the earth.
To turn aside the right of a man before the face of the most High,
'If you are willing to humble yourselves before the Lord and consent to the right, and walk humbly before Him, make it manifest by raising the right hand.'
To set up on high those that be low; that those which mourn may be exalted to safety.
A wicked man hardeneth his face: but as for the upright, he directeth his way.

Would to heaven there were no primacy, no eminence of place, and no
    tyrannical precedence of rank; that we might be known by eminence
    of virtue alone! But, as the case now stands, the distinction of a
    seat at the right hand or the left, or in the middle; at a higher
    or a lower place; of going before or aside of each other, has given
    rise to many disorders among us, to no…
Ye shall observe to do therefore as the Lord your God hath commanded you: ye shall not turn aside to the right hand or to the left.
To subvert a man in his cause, the Lord approveth not.
And said, O full of all subtilty and all mischief, thou child of the devil, thou enemy of all righteousness, wilt thou not cease to pervert the right ways of the Lord?
For he that in these things serveth Christ is acceptable to God, and approved of men.
For not he that commendeth himself is approved, but whom the Lord commendeth.
The foolishness of man perverteth his way: and his heart fretteth against the Lord.
¶ Who is he that saith, and it cometh to pass, when the Lord commandeth it not?
He that saith, I know him, and keepeth not his commandments, is a liar, and the truth is not in him.
Who am I, saith the Lord, that have promised and have not fulfilled?
Who am I that made man, saith the Lord, that will hold him guiltless that obeys not my commandments?
They said therefore, What is this that he saith, A little while? we cannot tell what he saith.
The Lord repented for this: It shall not be, saith the Lord.
Out of the mouth of the most High proceedeth not evil and good?
The heart of the righteous studieth to answer: but the mouth of the wicked poureth out evil things.
O generation of vipers, how can ye, being evil, speak good things? for out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.
See that none render evil for evil unto any man; but ever follow that which is good, both among yourselves, and to all men.
Shall evil be recompensed for good? for they have digged a pit for my soul. Remember that I stood before thee to speak good for them, and to turn away thy wrath from them.
They also that render evil for good are mine adversaries; because I follow the thing that good is.
Wherefore doth a living man complain, a man for the punishment of his sins?
A man of great wrath shall suffer punishment: for if thou deliver him, yet thou must do it again.

6 Bwwrtkai. The Vatican and Pal. render this, “ the various pun- 
ishments and tortures which men suffer daily in their lives.” Pseudo- 
‘Athanasius has: “ For when they revolt from God, thinking to be in 
rest and in wealth, then they are punished, some meeting with losses,” 
etc. [x Tim. i. 20. Remedial discipline is thus spoken of, x Cor.
And by reason hereof he ought, as for the people, so also for himself, to offer for sins.
¶ And if a man have committed a sin worthy of death, and he be to be put to death, and thou hang him on a tree:
Let us search and try our ways, and turn again to the Lord.
I the Lord search the heart, I try the reins, even to give every man according to his ways, and according to the fruit of his doings.
again in your midst. I ask the Lord to bless us, one and all, with his Holy Spirit, and to guide us in the way of life. Amen.
Seek the Lord and his strength, seek his face continually.
Let us lift up our heart with our hands unto God in the heavens.
Lift up your hands in the sanctuary, and bless the Lord.

We must watch and pray, and look well to our walk and conversation, and
live near to our God, that the love of this world may not choke the
precious seed of truth, and feel ready, if necessary, to offer up all
things, even life itself, for the Kingdom of Heaven's sake. 11:111.
Let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and our bodies washed with pure water.
We have transgressed and have rebelled: thou hast not pardoned.
And the Lord said, I have pardoned according to thy word:
And why dost thou not pardon my transgression, and take away mine iniquity? for now shall I sleep in the dust; and thou shalt seek me in the morning, but I shall not be.

¶ How shall I pardon thee for this? thy children have forsaken me, and sworn by them that are no gods: when I had fed them to the full, they then committed adultery, and assembled themselves by troops in the harlots’ houses.
Thou hast covered with anger, and persecuted us: thou hast slain, thou hast not pitied.
Thou, even thou, art to be feared: and who may stand in thy sight when once thou art angry?
For they persecute him whom thou hast smitten; and they talk to the grief of those whom thou hast wounded.
Thou hast clothed me with skin and flesh, and hast fenced me with bones and sinews.
Thou hast taken away all thy wrath: thou hast turned thyself from the fierceness of thine anger.

All thy lovers have forgotten thee; they seek thee not; for I have wounded thee with the wound of an enemy, with the chastisement of a cruel one, for the multitude of thine iniquity; because thy sins were increased.
Thou hast covered thyself with a cloud, that our prayer should not pass through.
Thou shalt ascend and come like a storm, thou shalt be like a cloud to cover the land, thou, and all thy bands, and many people with thee.
Thy mercy, O Lord, is in the heavens; and thy faithfulness reacheth unto the clouds.
With clouds he covereth the light; and commandeth it not to shine by the cloud that cometh betwixt.
For thy mercy is great above the heavens: and thy truth reacheth unto the clouds.
Canst thou lift up thy voice to the clouds, that abundance of waters may cover thee?
Thou hast made us as the offscouring and refuse in the midst of the people.
But thou hast utterly rejected us; thou art very wroth against us.
But thou hast saved us from our enemies, and hast put them to shame that hated us.
But thou hast cast off, and put us to shame; and goest not forth with our armies.
Thou makest us a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and a derision to them that are round about us.
As yet exaltest thou thyself against my people, that thou wilt not let them go?
All our enemies have opened their mouths against us.

All thine enemies have opened their mouth against thee: they hiss and gnash the teeth: they say, We have swallowed her up: certainly this is the day that we looked for; we have found, we have seen it.
Open thy mouth for the dumb in the cause of all such as are appointed to destruction.
For I will give you a mouth and wisdom, which all your adversaries shall not be able to gainsay nor resist.
And behold, we are again delivered out of the hands of our enemies. And blessed is the name of our God; for behold, it is he that has delivered us; yea, that has done this great thing for us.
Fear and a snare is come upon us, desolation and destruction.
Fear, and the pit, and the snare, are upon thee, O inhabitant of the earth.
The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the Lord shall be safe.
Therefore snares are round about thee, and sudden fear troubleth thee;
For as a snare shall it come on all them that dwell on the face of the whole earth.
The fear of the Lord is a fountain of life, to depart from the snares of death.
Mine eye runneth down with rivers of water for the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Mine eye affecteth mine heart because of all the daughters of my city.
Oh that my head were waters, and mine eyes a fountain of tears, that I might weep day and night for the slain of the daughter of my people!
For these things I weep; mine eye, mine eye runneth down with water, because the comforter that should relieve my soul is far from me: my children are desolate, because the enemy prevailed.
Mine eyes fail for thy salvation, and for the word of thy righteousness.
Mine eye trickleth down, and ceaseth not, without any intermission,
Mine eye is consumed because of grief; it waxeth old because of all mine enemies.
Mine eyes prevent the night watches, that I might meditate in thy word.
For mine eyes have seen thy salvation,
Till the Lord look down, and behold from heaven.

Lift up your eyes to the heavens, and look upon the earth beneath: for the heavens shall vanish away like smoke, and the earth shall wax old like a garment, and they that dwell therein shall die in like manner: but my salvation shall be for ever, and my righteousness shall not be abolished.

until you can “see as you are seen, and know as you are known.” Having commenced in the principles of truth and obtained the Spirit of light and intelligence that flows from God through obedience to the Gospel, it is for us to “purify ourselves even as God is pure,” and purge from ourselves all corruption, iniquity, fraud, lying and evil of every kind, all adultery, fornication, seduction and…
ever, and bestows His blessings upon all who will humble themselves before Him, and seek unto Him in the name of Jesus.
Mine eye affecteth mine heart because of all the daughters of my city.
Mine enemies chased me sore, like a bird, without cause.
I have pursued mine enemies, and overtaken them: neither did I turn again till they were consumed.
I have pursued mine enemies, and destroyed them; and turned not again until I had consumed them.
I was a reproach among all mine enemies, but especially among my neighbours, and a fear to mine acquaintance: they that did see me without fled from me.
When the wicked, even mine enemies and my foes, came upon me to eat up my flesh, they stumbled and fell.
Mine enemies reproach me all the day; and they that are mad against me are sworn against me.
They have cut off my life in the dungeon, and cast a stone upon me.
For indeed I was stolen away out of the land of the Hebrews: and here also have I done nothing that they should put me into the dungeon.
Lord, they have killed thy prophets, and digged down thine altars; and I am left alone, and they seek my life.
They have prepared a net for my steps; my soul is bowed down: they have digged a pit before me, into the midst whereof they are fallen themselves. Selah.
For when they had slain their children to their idols, then they came the same day into my sanctuary to profane it; and, lo, thus have they done in the midst of mine house.
Waters flowed over mine head; then I said, I am cut off.
And I said unto them that the water which my father saw was filthiness; and so much was his mind swallowed up in other things that he beheld not the filthiness of the water.
The waters compassed me about, even to the soul: the depth closed me round about, the weeds were wrapped about my head.
I have digged and drunk strange waters, and with the sole of my feet have I dried up all the rivers of besieged places.
Then the waters had overwhelmed us, the stream had gone over our soul:
¶ I called upon thy name, O Lord, out of the low dungeon.
Then called I upon the name of the Lord; O Lord, I beseech thee, deliver my soul.
O Lord, hear; O Lord, forgive; O Lord, hearken and do; defer not, for thine own sake, O my God: for thy city and thy people are called by thy name.
O Lord our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth!
¶ Again the word of the Lord came to me, saying,

If the place which the Lord thy God hath chosen to put his name there be too far from thee, then thou shalt kill of thy herd and of thy flock, which the Lord hath given thee, as I have commanded thee, and thou shalt eat in thy gates whatsoever thy soul lusteth after.
Thou hast heard my voice: hide not thine ear at my breathing, at my cry.
Surely thou hast spoken in mine hearing, and I have heard the voice of thy words, saying,
I cry unto thee, and thou dost not hear me: I stand up, and thou regardest me not.
Let my prayer come before thee: incline thine ear unto my cry;
For I said in my haste, I am cut off from before thine eyes: nevertheless thou heardest the voice of my supplications when I cried unto thee.
Thou drewest near in the day that I called upon thee: thou saidst, Fear not.
But I will deliver thee in that day, saith the Lord: and thou shalt not be given into the hand of the men of whom thou art afraid.
But there is forgiveness with thee, that thou mayest be feared.
Behold, my terror shall not make thee afraid, neither shall my hand be heavy upon thee.
For I feared thee, because thou art an austere man: thou takest up that thou layedst not down, and reapest that thou didst not sow.
O Lord, thou hast pleaded the causes of my soul; thou hast redeemed my life.
Into thine hand I commit my spirit: thou hast redeemed me, O Lord God of truth.
For their redeemer is mighty; he shall plead their cause with thee.
O Lord, wilt thou redeem my soul? Wilt thou deliver me out of the hands of mine enemies? Wilt thou make me that I may shake at the appearance of sin?
I will be glad and rejoice in thy mercy: for thou hast considered my trouble; thou hast known my soul in adversities;
O Lord, thou hast brought up my soul from the grave: thou hast kept me alive, that I should not go down to the pit.
O Lord, thou hast seen my wrong: judge thou my cause.
For thou hast maintained my right and my cause; thou satest in the throne judging right.
I know, O Lord, that thy judgments are right, and that thou in faithfulness hast afflicted me.
Wherefore I have not sinned against thee, but thou doest me wrong to war against me: the Lord the Judge be judge this day between the children of Israel and the children of Ammon.
O Lord, correct me, but with judgment; not in thine anger, lest thou bring me to nothing.
The Lord therefore be judge, and judge between me and thee, and see, and plead my cause, and deliver me out of thine hand.
Thou hast seen all their vengeance and all their imaginations against me.
Thou hast heard their reproach, O Lord, and all their imaginations against me;
But thou, O Lord, knowest me: thou hast seen me, and tried mine heart toward thee: pull them out like sheep for the slaughter, and prepare them for the day of slaughter.
And I will execute great vengeance upon them with furious rebukes; and they shall know that I am the Lord, when I shall lay my vengeance upon them.
And I will execute vengeance and fury upon them, even as upon the heathen, such as they have not heard.
Thou hast also given me the necks of mine enemies, that I might destroy them that hate me.
Thou hast heard their reproach, O Lord, and all their imaginations against me;
Thou hast seen all their vengeance and all their imaginations against me.

But when I speak with thee, I will open thy mouth, and thou shalt say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; He that heareth, let him hear; and he that forbeareth, let him forbear: for they are a rebellious house.

Yea, and thou hast also heard me when I have been cast out and have been despised by mine enemies; yea, thou didst hear my cries, and wast angry with mine enemies, and thou didst visit them in thine anger with speedy destruction.
And thou shalt speak my words unto them, whether they will hear, or whether they will forbear: for they are most rebellious.
The lips of those that rose up against me, and their device against me all the day.
Which my lips have uttered, and my mouth hath spoken, when I was in trouble.
Upon my right hand rise the youth; they push away my feet, and they raise up against me the ways of their destruction.
They also that seek after my life lay snares for me: and they that seek my hurt speak mischievous things, and imagine deceits all the day long.
They have gaped upon me with their mouth; they have smitten me upon the cheek reproachfully; they have gathered themselves together against me.
For mine enemies speak against me; and they that lay wait for my soul take counsel together,
Behold their sitting down, and their rising up; I am their musick.
They are brought down and fallen: but we are risen, and stand upright.
¶ Render unto them a recompence, O Lord, according to the work of their hands.
And let the beauty of the Lord our God be upon us: and establish thou the work of our hands upon us; yea, the work of our hands establish thou it.
Therefore hath the Lord recompensed me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands in his eyesight.
And thou shalt be blessed; for they cannot recompense thee: for thou shalt be recompensed at the resurrection of the just.
And render unto our neighbours sevenfold into their bosom their reproach, wherewith they have reproached thee, O Lord.
The Lord will perfect that which concerneth me: thy mercy, O Lord, endureth for ever: forsake not the works of thine own hands.
Give them sorrow of heart, thy curse unto them.
Let them curse, but bless thou: when they arise, let them be ashamed; but let thy servant rejoice.
Bless them that curse you, and pray for them which despitefully use you.
This we will do to them; we will even let them live, lest wrath be upon us, because of the oath which we sware unto them.
Let all their wickedness come before thee; and do unto them, as thou hast done unto me for all my transgressions: for my sighs are many, and my heart is faint.
Persecute and destroy them in anger from under the heavens of the Lord.
Destroy thou them, O God; let them fall by their own counsels; cast them out in the multitude of their transgressions; for they have rebelled against thee.
execute judgment upon them like the judgment of Sodom, and to leave no seed of the man on earth on the day of condemnation.
Let their way be dark and slippery: and let the angel of the Lord persecute them.
And in that place they shall lift up their voices unto God against that people, yea, unto him whose anger is kindled against their wickedness, a people who are well-nigh ripened for destruction.
